title: Roma Employment at the Turn of the Millennium. An Analysis of the Nationwide Representative Roma Survey of 2003
outlet: The Hungarian Labour Market 2010 (Fazekas, Lovász, Telegdy eds.)
summary_en: Chapter analysing Roma employment patterns at the turn of the millennium, drawing on the 2003 nationally representative Roma survey. Documents very large employment-rate gaps — Roma employment well below half the non-Roma rate, with the gap concentrated in low-skill, low-region settings. Most of the gap is explained by location, education, and the local-labour-market structure rather than direct labour-market discrimination alone.
policy_relevance: Foundational evidence for any Hungarian Roma-employment policy (e.g. Hídépítő, Útravaló, EU Roma Framework). Argues that education investment and local-labour-market activation are necessary conditions; anti-discrimination measures alone are insufficient. Still cited as the canonical Hungarian Roma labour-market reference. Caveat: the survey is now ~20 years old; a refresh on the 2018+ TÁRKI Roma sample would be valuable.
